page: Microsoft Azure E8pds_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine belonging to the Epdsv6 server family. It is powered by a Microsoft Neoverse-N2 processor with 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs running at 3.4 GHz. The architecture features 64 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 128 MB of L3 cache. This instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It includes 472 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage.

Benchmark data indicates strong memory subsystem performance, with the Passmark memory score ranking in the top tier (top 10%). Single-threaded gzip compression also registers in the top 25% at 33.15 GB/sec. Most general compute metrics, including stress-ng CPU performance, Geekbench multi-core, and Redis SET operations, fall within the average middle 50% range. However, cryptographic performance is a notable tradeoff, with the Passmark CPU encryption test scoring in the bottom 25%. Additionally, large language model prompt processing on a medium 7B model is slow, placing in the bottom 10% at 12.34 tokens/sec.

The ARM64 architecture provides a resource-dense profile that balances memory capacity and dedicated physical cores. This makes the server qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring high memory capacity without the overhead of hyperthreading. Given the 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and local storage, the instance is suited for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, caching layers, and web serving, though it is less optimal for heavy cryptographic or large-scale AI inference tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure E8pds_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine from the Epdsv6 family, featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs on a 3.4 GHz Microsoft Neoverse-N2 processor. It is configured with 64.0 GB of memory, yielding an 8.0 GB per core ratio, and includes 472 GB of local storage. Operating under Microsoft Hyper-V, this instance delivers top-tier memory performance, with its Passmark memory score ranking in the top 10% and single-threaded gzip compression in the top 25%. Conversely, cryptographic operations and medium-sized LLM prompt processing represent performance tradeoffs, scoring in the lower percentiles. The high memory density and dedicated physical cores make this server qualitatively cost-effective for memory-intensive applications, database operations, and caching layers, while avoiding the overhead of hyperthreading.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E8pds_v6 is an ARM64 virtual machine featuring 8 dedicated Neoverse-N2 cores, 64 GB of RAM, and 472 GB of local storage, offering top-tier memory performance for database workloads.
